title: Demolitions
description: Adds new explosives to your game!
body:
> ## Demoltions
### A mod about useful explosive items and blocks
![Demolitions banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f036b1c3ad9e97e4d0df3db587cd1601a4be2f42.png)

<center>

### Join our discord to stay up to date with mod updates, teasers, and more! (click)
[![Discord banner image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9cea9cd7dc8511b56c21d5eef30b040a9f9f81d2.png)](https://discord.gg/mEYDh4SUWN)

### Requires GeckoLib!
[![GeckoLib logo](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/197e417b0e192c49a53b39d7ad40764848f6959a.png)](https://modrinth.com/mod/geckolib)

</center>

# THIS MOD IS ABANDONED!
##### (If you want an alternative, use [BLAST](https://modrinth.com/mod/blast) instead.)

## Bombs

>#### Bomb

A classic bomb, made from iron, gunpowder and string, it has an explosive radius just below TNT and has a relatively fast cooldown. When most throwable explosives hit the floor or an entity, they have a timer until they explode. In this case, it's 2 seconds

![The bomb next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4360f076fcb0609428cf9e6ab59767afc8a4b289.png)

>#### Flaming Bomb

A bomb infused with flames, while you may think that it would set the block around it on fire, it isn't quite that simple. The Flaming Bomb gives inflicted enemies a type of fire called Flaming, and while you are flaming, you will be constantly set on fire, and will leave a trail of fire behind you as you walk. It has the same explosion radius, explosion timer and cooldown as the normal bomb, and is made with fire charges, gold ingots, and a normal bomb.

![The Flaming Bomb next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0f273a6f115f9ccef1420536054ad715dafdda9b.png)

>#### Shock Bomb

A bomb infused with lightning, it will summon large amounts of lightning wherever it explodes. These bombs have the same explosion radius and explosion timer as a normal bomb, but have a much higher cooldown. They are made with copper blocks, amethyst shards, and a normal bomb.

![The Shock Bomb next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/59aca513d73a2d1c44883fe91c2066d06da8ce83.png)

>#### Shrapnel Bomb

A bomb capable of launching sharp pieces of shrapnel around wherever it exploded. It has a smaller explosion radius, but is also faster. It has the same explosion timer as a normal bomb. It is made with iron ingots, iron nuggets, and a normal bomb.

![The Shrapnel Bomb next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c1a3dc8605d783d7d218fb68b9674fa93c210e7c.png)

>#### Cluster Bomb

A bomb that launches 4 smaller bombs when it explodes. It has the same explosion radius as a normal bomb, has a longer cooldown than a normal bomb, but still faster than the shock bomb. It also has a longer explosion timer, and is made with iron nuggets, gunpowder and shrapnel bombs

![The Cluster Bomb next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/341fbfe0322db64cbc3a279a97ce4e977b343856.png)

## Other Explosives

>#### Dynamite

A much more powerful piece of explosive, Dynamite has a large explosion radius, but at the cost of having a much higher cooldown. It is made with 1 paper, 2 gunpowder and 2 red dye. 3 Dynamite and 6 paper can be used to make a normal TNT block! But don't worry, you can also still make it the old way.

![A stick of dynamite next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/169bafe316e3b2584c59abed811322616ae1250c.png)

![The new TNT crafting recipe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/19444718539dfadc7c03dcf4a42cef17d6e9f81a.png)

>#### Cluster TNT

A variant of TNT which acts simularly to the cluster bomb, but more powerful. Acts in the same way as normal TNT, such as being activated by redstone or being activated by explosions. It is made with TNT and dynamite

![Some Cluster TNT next to some stone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ec6084d6e3cb881e075102a3c99c47943b7ecee4.png)

## Q&A

> Q: Will you update this?

A: No, it's abandoned.

> Q: Can i use this in my modpack?

A: I dont suggest it but sure.
